architecture.summary: PRMBench is a benchmark dataset and evaluation framework for process-level reward models (PRMs). It does not propose a novel PRM architecture; rather, it provides a systematic evaluation suite comprising 6,216 problems with 83,456 step-level labels designed to assess PRMs' fine-grained error detection capabilities across multiple dimensions (simplicity, soundness, sensitivity). The benchmark evaluates both open-source PRMs and LLMs prompted as critic models on their ability to detect various implicit error types in intermediate reasoning steps.

biblio.title: PRMBench: A Fine-grained and Challenging Benchmark for Process-Level Reward Models

claims.statement: PRMBENCH comprises 6,216 carefully designed problems and 83,456 step-level labels, evaluating models across multiple dimensions, including simplicity, soundness, and sensitivity.
Current benchmarks primarily focus on step correctness, failing to evaluate PRMs' performance systematically.
Experiments on 25 models, spanning across both open-source PRMs and LLMs prompted as critic models, uncover significant weaknesses in current PRMs.

PRMBench is specifically designed to assess the fine-grained error detection capabilities of PRMs, evaluating their ability to possess nuanced capabilities for detecting various implicit error types in real-world scenarios.
